preview_integration.overview: This file defines the mandatory pattern for creating advance preview files that integrate with IBM Business Automation Workflow's preview system. ALL widgets MUST follow this pattern.
preview_integration.critical_rule: NEVER create standalone preview implementations. ALL preview files MUST integrate with BAW's preview system using the mixObject pattern and BPMExt-Controls.preview.js layer.

file.name: WidgetName.html
file.purpose: Contains CSS styles and minimal container structure
file.content: - IBM copyright header - Embedded CSS styles scoped to the widget - Minimal HTML container (usually just a panel div) - NO hardcoded preview content - Preview content is generated by JavaScript
file.example: Widget Preview

file.name: WidgetNameSnippet.js
file.purpose: Implements BAW preview integration using mixObject pattern

method.name: createPreview
method.description: Main entry point for preview creation. Loads BAW preview layer, sets up Dojo modules, and generates preview content.
method.responsibilities: - Load BPMExt-Controls.preview.js from BAW - Require necessary Dojo modules - Create form group and label structure - Call generateSampleData() to create preview content - Execute callback when complete

method.name: getLabelDomElement
method.description: Returns the label DOM element for BAW to manipulate

method.name: generateSampleData
method.description: Creates the actual preview content using Dojo DOM manipulation. This is where widget-specific preview logic lives.
method.responsibilities: - Use domConstruct to create DOM elements - Use domClass to add CSS classes - Use domAttr to set attributes - Store references in this.context.coachViewData for updates - Create representative sample data for the widget

method.name: propertyChanged
method.description: Handles configuration option changes in the designer. Updates preview when user modifies widget config options.

method.name: modelChanged
method.description: Handles data binding changes (usually not needed for preview).

module.name: dojo/dom-construct
module.purpose: Create, place, and destroy DOM elements

module.name: dojo/dom-class
module.purpose: Manipulate CSS classes on DOM elements

module.name: dojo/dom-attr
module.purpose: Get and set DOM element attributes

widget.name: DateOutput
widget.description: Simple preview with formatted date display
widget.key_features: - Basic mixObject structure - Simple date formatting - Minimal DOM manipulation

widget.name: ProgressBar
widget.key_features: - Multiple DOM elements (track, fill, percentage, status) - Style attribute manipulation - propertyChanged() implementation for config options

widget.name: Stepper
widget.description: Complex preview with multiple interactive elements
widget.key_features: - Complex nested DOM structure - Multiple step items - Status-based styling

mistake.description: Creating standalone JavaScript functions without BAW integration
mistake.why_wrong: Does not integrate with BAW's preview system
mistake.correct_approach: Use mixObject pattern with createPreview() method

mistake.description: Hardcoding preview content in HTML file
mistake.why_wrong: Preview content must be generated dynamically by JavaScript
mistake.correct_approach: Generate content in generateSampleData() using Dojo modules

mistake.why_wrong: BAW preview system requires Dojo modules
mistake.correct_approach: Use domConstruct, domClass, domAttr from Dojo

mistake.why_wrong: Preview won't update when user changes config options in designer
mistake.correct_approach: Implement propertyChanged() to handle config option updates

mistake.description: Wrong file naming (e.g., Widget.js instead of WidgetSnippet.js)
mistake.why_wrong: BAW expects specific naming pattern for preview files
mistake.correct_approach: Always use WidgetNameSnippet.js for preview JavaScript

validation_checklist.item: Preview JavaScript file is named WidgetNameSnippet.js
Implements mixObject with all required methods
HTML file contains only styles and minimal container
Preview content is generated in generateSampleData()
No standalone JavaScript functions or vanilla DOM manipulation
Follows pattern from reference widgets (DateOutput, ProgressBar, Stepper)
